quality.rationale: 标准微分几何恒等式证明，依赖坐标展开与外代数性质，逻辑链条清晰且可推广至任意光滑流形

origin.question: Problem 8.21. If $\Omega$ is a p-form, show that $\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{X}}(\mathrm{d}\Omega) = \mathrm{d}(\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{X}}\Omega)$ .
origin.solution: Solution 8.21. Consider a 1-form $\tilde{\mathbf{U}} = \mathbf{U}_{\alpha}\widetilde{\mathrm{d}\mathbf{x}}^{\alpha}$ . First calculate $\mathrm{d}\mathcal{L}_{\mathbf{x}}^{\varrho}\tilde{\mathbf{U}}$$$\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \tilde {\mathbf {U}} = (\mathbf {x}^{\alpha} \mathbf {U}_{\beta , a} + \mathbf {U}_{a} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}, \beta) \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta}$$$$\mathbf {d} \left(\mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \tilde {\mathbf {U}}\right) = \left(\mathrm {x}^{\alpha} \mathrm {U}_{\beta , \alpha} + \mathrm {U}_{\alpha} \mathrm {x}^{\alpha}, \beta\right), \gamma \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta} \tag {1}$$Now calculate $\mathfrak{L}_{\mathbf{x}}(\tilde{\mathbf{d}\mathbf{U}})$$$\mathrm {d} \widetilde {\mathbf {U}} = \mathrm {U}_{\alpha , \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\alpha}$$$$\begin{array}{l} \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} (\mathrm {d} \tilde {\mathbf {U}}) = \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \left(\mathrm {U}_{a, \gamma}\right) \widetilde {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}}^{a} + \mathrm {U}_{a, \gamma} \mathcal {L}_{\mathbf {x}} \left(\widetilde {\mathrm {d} \mathbf {x}}^{\gamma}\right) \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{a} \\ + \mathrm {U}_{\alpha , \gamma} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \mathscr {L}_{\mathrm {x}} (\widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\alpha}) \\ = \mathrm {x}^{\tau} \mathrm {U}_{a, \gamma \tau} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{a} + \mathrm {U}_{a, \gamma} \mathrm {x}^{\gamma},_{\tau} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\tau} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{a} + \mathrm {U}_{a, \gamma} \mathrm {x}^{a},_{\tau} \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\tau} \\ = \left[ \left(\mathbf {x}^{\tau} \mathrm {U}_{\beta , \tau} + \mathrm {U}_{\alpha} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}, \beta\right), \gamma - \mathrm {U}_{\alpha} \mathbf {x}^{\alpha}, \beta \gamma\right] \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\gamma} \wedge \widetilde {\mathrm {d x}}^{\beta}. \tag {2} \\ \end{array}$$Equation (2) is obtained merely by relabeling indices. By symmetry of partial derivatives and antisymmetry of wedge product, the last term in brackets on the right hand side of Equation (2) vanishes. Comparing Equations (1) and (2), we have for 1-forms:$$\mathbf {d} \left(\mathscr {L}_{\mathbf {X}} \tilde {\mathbf {U}}\right) = \mathscr {L}_{\mathbf {X}} (\mathbf {d} \tilde {\mathbf {U}}).$$The generalization of this method from 1-form to p-form is straightforward.
